D5 Design L2 Understand Reference

Light-Dark Contrast

the most fundamental contrast of all — set two values on Itten's grey ladder and read the gap between them

Light-dark is the contrast that survives even in greyscale. Itten built a ladder of grey steps between white and black; any two colours can be compared by where they fall on it. The wider the value gap, the stronger the contrast — and the more legible the pairing.

Set the lighter and darker values. The ladder marks where each sits; the two large fields show the pairing. A small ΔL* reads as flat and low-contrast; a large one is crisp and bold.
